items.title: A Shock-based Interpretation of Radio and X-ray Emission in Active Galactic Nuclei
items.summary: arXiv:2607.02641v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose a shock-based framework to interpret the radio and X-ray emission in active galactic nuclei (AGNs), whose origin remains an open problem. In this framework, the radio emission is produced by synchrotron radiation in the a…

items.title: High-Energy Neutrino Tomography of the Earth's Interior with IceCube
items.summary: arXiv:2607.02644v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Earth's interior reflects its geological evolution, from accretion to present-day dynamics. Its structure drives the geodynamo in the outer core, generating the magnetic field that shields the surface from charged cosmic radiati…

items.title: First Search for Kaluza-Klein Gravitons and Radion Using Planck Data
items.summary: arXiv:2607.02651v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Heavy moduli and Kaluza-Klein (KK) gravitons from extra dimensions may evade terrestrial probes but can be produced during inflation, generating primordial non-Gaussianity (NG) through unavoidable couplings to density perturbations.…
